key_themes: Share price surge (~30% in late March) and holding gains
Potential for improved offer before 28 April deadline
Limited but speculative retail investor discussion
summary: BRCK Group plc (formerly Brickability), a UK building materials distributor, has generated positive sentiment after rejecting a non-binding 65p per share cash offer from Atlas Holdings as undervaluing the company.[[1]](https://www.insidermedia.com/news/south-east/brck-rejects-atlas-approach-as-fundamentally-undervaluing-company-but-leaves-door-open-for-higher-bid)[[2]](https://www.proactiveinvestors.com/companies/news/1089813/brck-group-shares-jump-29-after-board-rejects-65p-takeover-approach-from-atlas-holdings-1089813.html) Shares jumped sharply (~26-30%) on the late-March announcement and have largely held around 55-56p in mid-April trading, well above pre-bid levels near 42p.[[3]](https://uk.finance.yahoo.com/quote/BRCK.L/history/)[[4]](https://stockinvest.us/stock-price/BRCK.L) With the Takeover Panel deadline of 28 April 2026 approaching and ongoing regulatory disclosures (Form 8.3/8.5), investors are focused on whether a higher bid materializes or if the board's view of standalone value prevails. X/investor chatter is relatively sparse but shows speculative interest in an M&A outcome.[[5]](https://x.com/SharesMatt/status/2045262069352591841)[[6]](https://x.com/MHMembers/status/2039732938678440426)
sources: 10+ news articles and RNS filings on the Atlas approach/rejection (Mar-Apr 2026), 5-6 relevant investor X posts discussing the bid (mostly speculative), Yahoo Finance/LSE data showing price action around 55-56p in mid-April
